observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management see to it that compliance to related reportorial requirements on the utilization of public funds, such as BGCM, be complied with until the same is fully utilized/returned, to enable the constituents and oversight committees to obtain complete, timely, and transparent information on the full accountability on the use of such public funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the legalities required by law in the provision of funds for PPAs to be implemented, such as those provided for in Item 2.3 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 and Item 2.4 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 1, to support the validity of the transaction.

We recommended that Management see to it that utilizations out of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) be consistent with the approved LDRRMP as incorporated in the AIP in compliance with existing laws, rules, and regulations such as those provided for in Item 2.5.5 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated March 26, 2020, to support the legality of the transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to see to it that policies adopted concerning donations shall adhere with existing applicable accounting and reporting guidelines such as those provided for in Item II of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that all donations are properly accounted for.

We recommended that Management seek the required authority from the sanggunian concerned before any charges could be made against lump-sum appropriations, in adherence to Section 22.c of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527, to support the legality of the transactions.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the prescribed period set for the submission of Disbursement Voucher, together with pertinent supporting documents, for the cash advance for the release of Confidential Funds, pursuant to Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 to enable the concerned COA ATL to conduct a timely review of the transactions.

We recommended that Management religiously comply with all the reportorial requirements provided for the utilization of public funds, such as LGSF, for transparency of information consistent with the full disclosure policy required by the oversight committees.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Acting City Accountant to strictly adhere to the General Guidelines on the Fund Transfer to NGOs/POs set forth in Item 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, as well as observe compliance to the related documentary requirements prescribed in the said issuance to ensure the validity of the transaction.

We recommended and Management agreed to allocate funds for Maintenance and Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay for expenditures related to operations of the CDRRMO which shall be sourced from the General Fund of the City and refrain from charging against the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Update and finalize the Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in (3) three months or less,
Prepare RPCPPE for CY 2021 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and
Submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to the Office of the Auditor at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking activities, and ensure implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed, through the head of the implementing offices, to ensure the full and prompt implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the 20% EDF by addressing the causes of the delay in order to deliver timely the optimum benefits to the constituents of the City as envisioned in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management revisit the MOA and ensure the compliance of both parties to the terms and conditions contained in it to ensure the realization of the goals and objectives of the related programs/projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct:

a) The Acting City Treasurer to report the collection and deposit through RCD on the date it was actually collected and deposited;
	
b) The Acting City Accountant to observe the proper recording of collections and deposits in order to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information; and

c) The Acting City Accountant to adjust the erroneous entry to correct the accounts affected.
observations.managementAction: Complied fully with the recommendation.

c.	Direct the CAO to draw the necessary JEV on the basis of the submitted report to recognize the issuance and correspondingly reflect the correct balance of the inventory account.

observations.recommendation: 1.9	We recommend Management to instruct the City Accountant to establish the probability of collections of the long outstanding receivables including the determination of the location of the responsible persons or NGOs/POs. Issue demand letters, if applicable, and if collection is nil, request the write-off of the dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005. Finally, disclose to the Notes to the Financial Statements to present fairly and free of material misstatements the financial statements of the City of Bislig.

2.9	We recommend Management to require the City Accounting Office conduct a detailed review on the paid payrolls in previous years, and if the amount was determined and established require the City Treasurer’s Office to pay the amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. In addition, require the Human Resource Office to review the master list of all employees in previous years. In addition, send a confirmation letter to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to determine and validate the outstanding/unremitted contributions/premiums/loan repayments withheld.

3.8	We recommend that Management to fast-track the registration of lands under the name of the City Government of Bislig so that its ownership will be conclusive, guaranteed to be indefeasible, unassailable, and imprescriptible. Further, instruct the City Accountant to reconcile the record of Land account with the RPCPPE.

4.5	We recommend that City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s Office to continue the observance of the internal control put in place to continually enhance the presentation of its current and long-term obligations in the Financial Statements.

5.5	We recommend that Management direct the City Accountant to finish as soon as possible the verification made to be able to prepare and process the necessary disbursement relative to the outstanding PDAF funds which is in the possession of LGU-Bislig City and thereafter, instruct the City Treasurer to immediately deposit the said funds to the account of the Bureau of Treasury.

6.6	We recommend Management to instruct the personnel in-charge for the preparation of payroll to prioritize the withholding and remittance of loan amortizations due to GSIS, and to observe the mandated net take home pay for government personnel pursuant to Section 48 of RA No. 10964.

7.4 	We recommend that Management instruct the City Accounting Office to thoroughly examine the BIR registration documents of the job orders, contract of services and casual personnel, and to be updated and knowledgeable on new rules and regulations on the collection, withholding and remittance of tax in order to collect and withhold the correct amount of tax.

8.6	We recommend Management to instruct the City Treasurer to issue notice of delinquencies and/or effect appropriate administrative action for the collection of the delinquent real property tax with the assistance of the Sanggunian, if necessary, to all delinquent real properties particularly to those delinquents for a long period of time. In addition, address the manpower requirement of the City Treasurer’s Office.

9.7	We recommend that Management instruct the City Accountant to continue the withholding of refund from the payroll and other claims of the liable personnel from audit disallowances which became final and executory. In addition, immediately report the settlement made for the issuance of Notice of Settlement of Suspension, Disallowances and Charges (NSSDC).

10.6	We recommend Management that intensive information and education campaign on ESWM be conducted, close coordination with the CESWM and BESWM Boards on institutionalizing effective policies on solid waste management, provision of necessary appropriation for the operation of controlled dump, and consider the provision of incentives pursuant to Section 45 Chapter IV of RA No. 9003. In addition, fast track the completion of Terms of Reference on the construction of sanitary landfill.

11.5	We recommend that Management instruct the concerned barangay officials to retrieve the necessary documents for the implemented programs and projects under the fund transfer and submit to the CAO for immediate liquidation. In addition, instruct the CEO to fast track the preparation of POW, and the issuance of the Certificate of Completion for completed projects. Finally, consider the manpower requirement in the CEO.

12.6	We recommend that LGU GFPS include in its GPB the capacity development of its members particularly familiarizing the HGDG tool in conducting gender analysis for the city’s PPAs to be attributed to GAD, and to observe the guidelines on the submission, review and approval of the GPB and GAD AR to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office.

13.6	We recommend that Management instruct the City Treasurer to include on its list of deficient real property tax the reasons of non-collection and non-payment to institute appropriate and effective policies for the collection of the real property tax. In addition, the City Accountant to recognize allowance for impairment and disclosure to the Notes of the FS for these receivable accounts due to the identified observations.

14.9	We recommend that LDRRMO fast-track the procurement and/or implementation of these PPAs and prepare work and financial plan to monitor the timeline of its implementation. In addition, consider the manning requirement of the CDRRM Department for the efficient and effective implementation of PPAs in the LDRRMF.

15.6	We recommend that concerned offices fast-track the preparation of the POW, the timetable for the implementation of these PPAs, and the procurement process. In addition, require the barangay governments to liquidate previous fund transfers.

16.7	We recommend that concerned departments prepare a project monitoring plan and/or work and financial plan for every PPAs to be implemented either locally sourced or from NGAs through fund transfers to monitor the timely implementation and completion of these PPAs efficiently. Also, process the return of unused balances from completed PPAs to the concerned NGAs.

2. Series of meetings were held related to review and evaluation of the PPM facilities

3. Proposed agreements/resolution of the issue is for finalization
observations.reason: As of this writing, there was no MOA/contract was issued to that effect to resolve the issues in the operation of Mangagoy Public Birthing Facility Inc. However, draft receipts of the operation were deposited to the LGU under the TF account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall direct the CDRRM Officer to prepare a well- planned LDRRMFIP containing all the necessary data and information that will facilitate the evaluation and review of the LDRRMP in relation to the national, regional and provincial planning programs.  We also recommended that the LDRRMC shall conduct periodic meetings with the CDRRMO to assess and monitor the implementation of the LDRRMP to ensure that the DRRMF is fully utilized in the implementation of PPAs that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the City to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its populace stricken by calamities.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2017 AAR | 1. Prepare and submit PPAs to be approved by the CDRRM Council

2. The CDRRMC will create a Technical Working Committee, solely to monitor the implementation of CDRRM Projects

3. Continuing programs

4. Movable and deployable Resource Unit and IT Equipment

5. Purchase of Life saving equipment, DRRM Rescue Ambulance, Relief Operation Vehicle.

6. Purchase of Fire Truck

7. DRRM Mitigation Project

8. Purchase of Evacuation Lot
observations.reason: There were still PPAs that were not implemented and/or delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the GAD Focal Point System to carefully examine the GAD Plan in accordance with the prescribed processes and procedures and to formulate gender-responsive PPAs to address gender issues and concerns based on a gender analysis undertaken using the sex-disaggregated data. Moreover, Management should also allocate in the authorized GAD Plan and Budget the PPAs that are mainstreamed in various offices and properly align it with the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2017 AAR | 1. Establish sex disaggregated data and evaluation of LGU PPAs implementation

2. Strengthen the City GAD Focal Point System through creation of positions for GAD Focal Persons, Capability Building of GFPS to enrich understanding in gender roles and relation in a specific context, monthly meeting of GFPS and GAD issues and concerns to all agencies, provisions of TA to all concerned agencies, monitoring and evaluation of GAD Program Implementation
observations.reason: The LGU is yet to established its sex disaggregated data. In addition, coordination of offices is needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall develop structured M & E system with appropriate tools, and a reporting system on the progress/status of PPAs using performance indicators, and targets and baselines, where applicable, with an analysis of expected outputs, objectives and goals. Moreover, Management shall conduct regular meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Management initiated the conduct of planning and workshop to establish the M & E system and regular reporting of the LGU in accordance with the timelines set for the completion of the various PPAs.
observations.reason: Assessment of the monitoring and evaluation system (M&E) and reporting of the PPAs was not established by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to reclassify the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Time Deposit under the line item of Investment in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statements in all bank accounts of the City to show the correct cash in bank balances reflected in the Statement of Financial Position. Further, remind the City Accountant to observe the legal deadline on the submission of bank reconciliation statements to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to review the applicable accounting standard on the recognition principle of the property, plant, and equipment. Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant disclose the missing PPE in the notes to financial statements to provide adequate information.

We recommended that the City General Service Officer send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the production of the missing government properties and/or report explaining why they were not found during the physical count. Also, advise the accountable officer to request for relief from accountability to COA for the lost items within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Engineer to consistently furnish the City Accountant the report of all completed projects which serves as basis in the recording of adjustment/reclassification of the accounts. Further, direct the City Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry for the recognition of the assets accompanying the completion of the said projects pursuant to the provisions of Section 50 of the Manual of NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended Management to instruct the City Accountant that the uncollected/outstanding rental/lease on properties owned or administered by the City Government of Bislig be recognized and recorded as receivables and income in accordance with PPSAS 1.7 on accrual basis. Likewise, direct the CEEDO Manager to submit the monthly delinquency report of stalls and its details to the City Accounting Office for proper recording and monitoring of the unpaid rentals/leases.

We recommended that Management expedite the enactment of the revised City Revenue Code to provide a current tax rates that would boost the income generation of the city. We also recommend that the existing articles and provisions be revisited with end-in-view of considering the call of the present times towards the best interest of the public and the government.

We recommended the Local Development Council, in coordination with the local officials, civil society organizations and other stakeholders, to carefully identify PPAs that are specific in nature covering the complete description of the project, location, intended beneficiaries and estimated cost of the PPAs. Further, we recommended to secure concurrence from the Local Sanggunian when the appropriated amounts indicated in the Annual Budget and AIP were stated in generic term before the implementation of the PPAs to clothe them with legal authority.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer to coordinate with the Acting City Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund on monthly basis in order to submit the said report to the concerned offices on the due dates pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 -002.

We recommended Management to strictly adhere the full implementation of RA No. 9003 and hasten the establishment of sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the City’s constituents from the possible ecological impact.

We recommended the Local School Board to prioritize in the formulation of SEF budget the construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities pursuant to Item 4.1.2.1 of the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 dated January 19, 2017 in order to provide improved academic environment and basic education services.

We recommended Management to stop the practice of charging ineligible expenditures against the Special Education Fund and to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and other regulations governing the utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that Management through its GFPS facilitate the conduct of gender analysis using the HGDG for proper identification of gender-responsive PPAs in the GPB and to undertake the same analysis on its prepared GAD AR to determine the actual expenditures that can be attributed to GAD budget.

We recommended that Management through its GFPS first submit the GPB and GAD AR to the PPDO to ensure alignment of priority PPAs of the province. Then to the DILG Provincial Office for review and endorsement to be incorporated in the LGU annual budget.

We recommended Management to submit the quarterly report on publicized government projects/programs/activities providing all the vital information pursuant to COA Circular Number 2013-004. Also, we recommended that Management direct the City Planning Office in coordination with the City Engineering Office to act as the consolidator of the reports submitted by the different departments/offices of the LGU on the status of PPAs implementation.

We recommended that Management assess the impact of the significant delays in the procurement process of the city and introduce improvements in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and services. Furthermore, direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer, City Treasurer, and end-users on the basic and additional documents for all transactions to facilitate on time processing of the DVs and establish tracking system to easily monitor the flow of the transaction.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and process the necessary disbursement voucher relative to the outstanding PDAF funds in the possession of LGU-Bislig City and instruct the City Treasurer to immediately return the said funds to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended Management to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), City Planning and Development Office and the City Engineering Office to review the composition of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) on the on-going procurements and apply the new VAT rate in the computation of project cost pursuant to the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 22 Series of 2015 for the future projects.

We recommended that Management direct all department heads involved in the implementation of PPAs to review all plans and timetables and monitor the utilization of the 20% development fund to ensure that the allocated funds are optimally used according to its intended purpose. Moreover, instruct the implementing office/s to expedite the implementation of the projects and appraise the viability of the projects which are not yet implemented during the year for appropriate reversion of appropriations duly approved by the Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to formulate comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan and to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Panlungsod, through Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local Development Council, the annual LDRRMO plan and budget indicating the proposed programming of the current LDRRM Fund and Special Trust Fund- DRRM to ensure that the available LDDRM funds are used to strengthen the local disaster risk reduction management. Moreover, establish a structured monitoring system to track down the implementation of various PPAs for DRRM whether these were achieved according to its expected results.

We recommended that Management shall direct the City Accountant and General Services Officer to render an accounting of the result of recently conducted physical inventory treating the disparity of the two records.
observations.managementAction: Issued a directive to the City Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile their respective records to arrive a reconciled balance of the PPE.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going despite that the physical inventory taking of PPE had already been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall direct the City Accountant to properly recognize the small items purchased for disaster response and rescue activities under its appropriate inventory accounts to fairly present the balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant was directed to comply the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Recognition of small items purchased for disaster response and rescue activities was strictly observed by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall revisit its Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan and ensure that the identified programs, projects and activities are compliant to Item 3.23 of Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: Revisited the POP for 2017 and prepared three- year Public Safety Plan in accordance to the existing laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Management revisited their POP and promised to prepare the PPAs according Item 3.23 of Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

observations.managementAction: Management issued directive to endorse all procurement of consulting services to the BAC in compliance to RA 9184. Furthermore, issued an executive order creating a committee responsible to oversee and evaluate the qualification of consultants.
observations.reason: Management created a committee to oversee the hiring of consultants and its standard process. Likewise, the BAC observed the requirements in RA 9184 on the purchase of consultancy services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide in the TOR/contract with each consultant the detailed scope of work to be rendered, expected outputs/deliverables, delivery schedules/timeline, and working time, appropriate, and all the requisites of a consulting services contract to safeguard the interest of the city government.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed the Human Resource Management Officer to adopt the audit recommendation by establishing clear term of reference (TOR) in the hiring of consultants.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the term of reference in the contract of services disclosed clear parameters of consultants’ expected deliverables and its duties and responsibilities in the consultancy engagement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall develop a standard process for the evaluation of consultants’ services with the consideration whether their services require technical and professional expertise which could not be undertaken by the City Government employees and that the costs outweighed the benefits and set criteria for the renewal. Moreover, Management shall require the concerned heads of departments/offices with the assistance of the HRMO to evaluate the recommendations for rehiring of the consultants’ services to ensure that the renewal is necessary and based on criteria set prior to recommendation for approval by the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer, in coordination with the heads of concerned offices, conducted a meeting to evaluate the consultants’ outputs and draft guidelines on the renewal of their contact of services.
observations.reason: Management undertook the evaluation of consultant’s output and proper documentation was faithfully established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management shall:
a. Assess the impact of the significant delays in the procurement process of the city in coordination with the concerned offices and introduce improvements in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and services.
b. Instruct the GSO to revisit the procurement rules and documentary requirements taking into consideration the timelines set by RA 9184; and
c. Direct the City Accountant to closely coordinate with the GSO on the basic and additional documents for all transactions to facilitate the processing of the DVs.
observations.managementAction: Management issued memorandum directing all concerned offices to strictly observe the deadline on the submission of PPMPs and mandatory review of all purchase request before its submission to City Budget Office.
observations.reason: Acquisition of common supplies, fuel and lubricants, and other office equipment was delivered on time. However, implementation of infrastructure projects was significantly delayed due to the complexities of procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall strictly adhere to Section 325(a) of RA 7160 to ensure equitable distribution of government scarce resources towards the upliftment of the socio-economic condition of its people. Moreover, the city should formulate a comprehensive human resource management plan to ensure that the hiring of manpower is necessary for the city’s operation and the piecemeal creation of new plantilla positions can be avoided since it entails additional PS funding requirements.
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed the organizational structure and formulated the Human Resource Development Plan to serve as reference in creating additional positions.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, LGU-Bislig City did not appoint some of the vacant positions to ensure the excess amount of PS will not be disbursed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council (LDC) shall identify priority projects in accordance with Section 3 of the JMC and consider only those projects/programs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures to ensure that the 20% development fund was made available for its primary purpose.
observations.managementAction: PPAs funded by 20% EDF shall strictly conform to the guidelines of the utilization of 20% EDF. Reviewed of the PPAs was initiated by the sectoral committees of the City Development Council.
observations.reason: There were still PPAs which do not conform to the rules and regulations of 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall stop the practice of allowing the payment of honoraria to LPRAT members. We further recommended that Management shall refrain from granting honorarium or any form of personnel benefits without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive issued memorandum for the stoppage of granting honoraria to the LPRAT members.
observations.reason: There were no honoraria granted to LPRAT members in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management shall ensure that proper segregation and storage of solid waste pending for collection is in accordance to Section 22 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued memorandum to all barangay captains to comply the recommendation.  Monitoring on the implementation and issuance of citation tickets to non-compliant stakeholders were undertaken by the LGU.
observations.reason: Validation of the management action disclosed that some MRF contained of improperly segregated wastes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management shall ensure the compliance on the standards and requirements for collection and transport of solid waste pursuant to Section 24 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Installed net to all units engaged in solid waste collection.
observations.reason: The vehicles of the City assigned to collect solid wastes were installed net to ensure that the wastes are intact.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management shall strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003. 
To address the challenges in the existing sanitary landfill the City shall:
a. Maintain the drainage system to ensure proper flow of water;
b. Provide cover procedure befitting the existing condition using landfill equipment to promote lateral runoff of precipitation and to prevent ponding. Grades shall be established of sufficient slopes to account for future settlement of the fill surface; and
c. Maintain access road to facilitate better transport of collected solid waste.
observations.managementAction: Assigned one (1) unit backhoe at the Eco-Park to reinforce the old aged bulldozer. Rehabilitation of the access road and improvement of residual containment area were also implemented in CY 2017.
observations.reason: Validation of the site revealed that the residual containment area is not fully compliant to the standard.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the that City Accounting Office shall send follow up demand letters requiring the submission of the Fund Utilization/Liquidation Report from NGOs/POs with outstanding balances to ascertain that funds granted were utilized in accordance with Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).  Moreover, Management shall direct the Program coordinators of the projects to monitor the compliance per memorandum of agreement to guarantee that the fund transfers were utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: Management directed the City Accountant to send follow up demand letters to all NGOs/POs which did not submit utilization/liquidation reports. Likewise, program coordinators were instructed to monitor the implementation of the programs according to the MOA.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, only few POs/NGOs submitted the utilization/liquidation reports to the City Accounting Office. Programs coordinators failed to coordinate the City Accounting Office on the status of project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to the defaulting NGOs/POs/Individuals to settle their obligations on the financial assistance granted and consistently monitor the submission of the Fund Utilization reports of all NGOs/POs/ Individuals and to promptly record the liquidations in the books to ensure the accuracy of the balances of the amount reported in the financial statements.
We also recommended that Management shall direct the heads of implementing offices in coordination with the City Treasurer and City Accountant to develop strategies and submit an action plan to recover the ₱5,878,154.50 long overdue outstanding loans detailing the activities to be undertaken, the person(s) to be assigned to collect and the target period of recovery.
observations.managementAction: Required the concerned offices such as the City Cooperative Development Office, City Agriculture Office, and City Social Welfare and Development to take action on the collection. Demand letters were sent to the recipients.
observations.reason: Verification of the collection on the repayments of financial assistance to individuals/cooperatives remained very low.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to assign an accounting staff to religiously prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis. Likewise, Management shall require the CDRRM Officer to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the CDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA Auditor as required in COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management directed CDRRM Officer to coordinate with the City Accounting Office for the monthly report on the sources and utilization of the DRRM funds which will be submitted to LDRRMC and other oversight bodies.
observations.reason: Review of the submission on the monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRM funds revealed that CDRRM Office submitted only the report first quarter of CY 2017 and no reports thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall stop the practice of realigning the current appropriations to augment the deficient appropriations of some items in the SEF budget. LSB is reminded to observe proper planning to guarantee that the SEF will be used for the purpose it was created.
observations.managementAction: Planned the priority needs of schools based on the recommendation of the school heads. Proposed budget for CY 2017 was presented to the Local School Board during the LSB meeting which was eventually approved by the board.
observations.reason: The Local School Board did not revert any current appropriation in CY 2017 budget to augment any deficient appropriation/s.  Moreover, participation of the school heads in the planning process was solicited to come up with CY 2017 SEF budget that caters the priority needs of the public elementary and secondary schools.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the GAD Focal Point System to carefully examine the GAD Plan in accordance with the prescribed processes and procedures and to formulate gender-responsive PPAs to address gender issues and concerns based on a gender analysis undertaken using the sex-disaggregated data. Moreover, Management should also allocate in the authorized GAD Plan and Budget the PPAs that are mainstreamed in various offices and properly align it with the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: Established sex disaggregated data of the LGU and strengthened the City GAD focal point system through conducting capacity building programs and seminars. Moreover, the City revisited the PPAs that were included in the GAD Plan and budget whether they are GAD responsive.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall revisit or carefully study the legality and propriety of the operation to ensure that the interest of the government is amply protected. Further, we recommended that Management shall execute a Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the authority of the Sangguniang Panlungsod that clearly defines the responsibilities and accountabilities of both parties to guarantee that the operation is clothed with legal authority.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a memorandum dated January 19, 2017 designating evaluation team for the operations of the Mangagoy Birthing Facility. Series of meetings were held to review and evaluate the operation of the facility. Proposed agreements and resolutions were crafted but subject for finalization.
observations.reason: As of this writing, there was no memorandum of agreement/contract was issued to that effect to resolve the issues in the operation of Mangagoy Public Birthing Facility Inc. However, draft receipts of the operation were deposited to the LGU under the trust fund account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the maximum utilization of the 20% development fund so that infrastructure projects are efficiently and effectively implemented during the year to provide socio-economic development.

We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) shall thoroughly plan the annual procurement activities and stop the practice of conducting public bidding for infrastructure projects in the last quarter of the year to preclude the delayed implementation of the projects. Moreover, the city government should strengthen its monitoring of the projects so that appropriate information on the status of the projects will be given to the public and other stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Office was directed to expedite the preparation of detailed engineering design and program of work as basis for the Bids and Awards to take their appropriate action. Furthermore, the BAC was instructed to prepare an annual schedule of public bidding with due consideration of the climate condition of the City.
observations.reason: Some of the developmental projects were not yet completed or started for implementation as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall direct the CDRRM Officer to prepare a well- planned LDRRMFIP containing all the necessary data and information that will facilitate the evaluation and review of the LDRRMP in relation to the national, regional and provincial planning programs.  We also recommended that the LDRRMC shall conduct periodic meetings with the CDRRMO to assess and monitor the implementation of the LDRRMP to ensure that the DRRMF is fully utilized in the implementation of PPAs that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the City to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its populace stricken by calamities.
observations.managementAction: Management created a technical working committee to solely monitor the implementation of CDRRM projects. Assigned focal persons from GSO and CDRRMO to handle the processing of required documents relative to the disaster projects and activities.
observations.reason: Verification of the PPAs of CDRRMO revealed that there were several projects and capital outlays not implemented as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall involve members of the City Development Council and other stakeholders to give their inputs to the city’s planning process to ensure that development issues identified represent the general concern and priorities of the stakeholders, and a consensus is arrived at on the prioritization of the issues for resolution and inclusion in the City Development Plan.
observations.managementAction: The City Development Council prepared notices together with the minutes of the previous meeting to the CDC members and stakeholders. Meta cards were given to the CDC members and stakeholders to encourage them to participate during the planning process Moreover, establishment of clear criteria for purpose of prioritization and coming up with consensus decision on the identified PPAs was introduced.
observations.reason: The LGU-Bislig City furnished the Audit Team with documents relative to the conducted meetings of City Development Council during the planning stage to identify priority PPAs for the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall group/cluster the department/offices by sector to establish collaboration/linkages and accountability for implementation of PPAs thus ensuring the attainment of the sector’s objectives and goals. Moreover, we recommended that Management shall evaluate and identify a lead department/office for each sector to spearhead planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, and reporting on each sector’s PPAs, objectives and goals.
observations.managementAction: Conducted planning workshop to different clusters with the emphasis on planning, monitoring, evaluation and reporting of PPAs of the City in accordance with its goals, objectives and targets.
observations.reason: Collaboration of the different clusters was effective in the planning of PPAs in the ensuing year which helped them to avoid the duplication of programs, projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall fast track the updating of the Comprehensive Land Used Plan and prepare its City Development Plan after analyses of its stakeholders, development problems and objectives and strategies anchored on the principle of participatory planning process.
observations.managementAction: The City Development Council adopted the Comprehensive Land Use and City Development Plan but subject for review by the Local Sanggunian.
observations.reason: As of this writing, Management was unable to furnish the Audit Team copy of the CLUP and CDP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall adopt a structured planning process in order to develop a logical framework (log frame) indicating the elements which are appropriately and correctly stated/presented to facilitate measurement of progress/change. Further, utilize the log frame in the development of its next CDP as well as investment program and budget, and in monitoring, evaluating and reporting on accountability and results.
observations.managementAction: Management required the proponents of all project proposal to prepare a brief description detailing the information, input, output, resources, manpower requirement and impact to establish monitoring and reporting of PPAs.
observations.reason: The City of Bislig introduced a simple structured planning process that could measure the progress of project implementation.

observations.managementAction: Management did not submit the course of action to comply the recommendation.
observations.reason: They failed to prepare and submit the accountability reports to the Local Finance Committee and other oversight bodies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the BUB focal persons to coordinate and request assistance from the national line agencies in the implementation of projects and to strictly observe the timely submission of liquidation reports to hasten the downloading of funds to the LGU.   Further, instruct the City Planning Officer to hold consultative meeting with the implementing offices on the formulation of strategies that will enable the city to implement all nationally and locally funded projects within the timeframe.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit course of action on how to hasten the implementation of nationally and locally funded projects.
observations.reason: A number of locally and nationally funded projects remained unimplemented as of December 31, 2016.

Further, we recommended that Management shall execute a Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the authority of the Sangguniang Panlungsod that clearly defines the responsibilities and accountabilities of both parties to guarantee that the operation is clothed with legal authority.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the maximum utilization of the 20% development fund so that infrastructure projects are efficiently and effectively implemented during the year to provide socio-economic development.
We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) shall thoroughly plan the annual procurement activities and stop the practice of conducting public bidding for infrastructure projects in the last quarter of the year to preclude the delayed implementation of the projects. Moreover, the city government should strengthen its monitoring of the projects so that appropriate information on the status of the projects will be given to the public and other stakeholders.

We recommended that Management shall direct the CDRRM Officer to prepare a well- planned LDRRMFIP containing all the necessary data and information that will facilitate the evaluation and review of the LDRRMP in relation to the national, regional and provincial planning programs.  We also recommended that the LDRRMC shall conduct periodic meetings with the CDRRMO to assess and monitor the implementation of the LDRRMP to ensure that the DRRMF is fully utilized in the implementation of PPAs that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the City to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its populace stricken by calamities.

We recommended that Management shall involve members of the City Development Council and other stakeholders to give their inputs to the city’s planning process to ensure that development issues identified represent the general concern and priorities of the stakeholders, and a consensus is arrived at on the prioritization of the issues for resolution and inclusion in the City Development Plan.

We recommended that Management shall group/cluster the department/offices by sector to establish collaboration/linkages and accountability for implementation of PPAs thus ensuring the attainment of the sector’s objectives and goals. Moreover, we recommended that Management shall evaluate and identify a lead department/office for each sector to spearhead planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, and reporting on each sector’s PPAs, objectives and goals.

We recommended that Management shall develop structured M & E system with appropriate tools, and a reporting system on the progress/status of PPAs using performance indicators, and targets and baselines, where applicable, with an analysis of expected outputs, objectives and goals. Moreover, Management shall conduct regular meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.

We recommended that Management shall fast track the updating of the Comprehensive Land Used Plan and prepare its City Development Plan after analyses of its stakeholders, development problems and objectives and strategies anchored on the principle of participatory planning process.

We recommended that Management shall adopt a structured planning process in order to develop a logical framework (log frame) indicating the elements which are appropriately and correctly stated/presented to facilitate measurement of progress/change. Further, utilize the log frame in the development of its next CDP as well as investment program and budget, and in monitoring, evaluating and reporting on accountability and results.

We recommended that the City Accountant send follow-up letters to the bank requesting for the credit and debit memos to support the required adjustments. 

Further, the Accountant is advised to prepare journal entry vouchers taking up in the books the identifiable reconciling items to ensure the accuracy of the balances of the affected accounts in the financial records of the City.
observations.managementAction:
City Accountant sent follow -up letter and made personal representation with the bank. 

Prepare separate journal entry.
observations.reason: Verification disclosed that the City Accountant communicated the bank requesting for the debit and credit memos as basis in the preparation of journal entry voucher to effect adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the City Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and payables accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus ensuring the presentation of reliable data on the financial reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Directed City Accountant to make adjustments of entries during the BADGE meeting.
observations.reason: The intra-agency receivables and payable s were already reconciled as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the City Accountant and General Services Officer to hasten the reconciliation of property records and to account for the discrepancy of ₱30,217,245.99. Moreover, management should also instruct the GSO to complete the inventory taking and submit inventory reports to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
Conduct continuous reconciliation of records. 

Lack of technical manpower to conduct inventory.

Continuation of inventory and reconciliation of records for completion by December 2016.
observations.reason: There are still unreconciled balances of PPE between the accounting records and the physical inventory reports. However, this could not fairly state the balances of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:
a. Direct the City Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as one of the basic document supporting fund transfers pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001 and to record all fund transfers as receivables to monitor the utilization of the fund; 
b. Direct the City Budget Office and City Accountant not to process claims with insufficient appropriations in compliance to Section 305 of RA 7160; and 
c. Direct the City Accountant to require the VMLP-SDS Chapter to submit their liquidation reports on the amounts transferred to them.
observations.managementAction:
Issue directive to concerned offices to comply with COA recommendations   as to requirements and not to process similar claims without complying with the COA findings.

No more processing of similar transfers without the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: Management strictly adhere to the requirement of MOA before the funds were transferred to other government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accounting Office strictly adhere to Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 in the submission of accounts to enable us to make timely review of the transactions and deficiencies, if any, can be communicated immediately to management. We also recommended that the City Treasurer forward the paid disbursement vouchers weekly and to indicate in her transmittal letter the DVs that cannot be forwarded because the payees have not claimed yet their checks.
observations.managementAction: Submission by the City Accountant every 10th day of the succeeding month and by the City treasurer on the following week for on time fast tracking.
observations.reason: The City Accounting Office exerted efforts to submit the financial reports on time. However, there were some reports submitted way beyond the legal time frame due to the voluminous transactions of the city.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of paying personal benefits through cash advance and direct the accountable officers to deposit the allowances and personnel benefits to the ATM accounts of the individual city officials and employees to protect public funds from losses through man made and unforeseen events.
observations.managementAction: Directed the concerned department heads during BADGE meeting to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: All the benefits and allowances were deposited to the individual ATM account of the city officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strengthen its planning process and stop the practice of making augmentations through realignment of appropriation. Moreover, instruct the City Budget Officer to review and evaluate thoroughly the budget proposals of the different departments/offices to avoid frequent augmentation of expense items found to be deficient during the year.
observations.managementAction:
Inform/ remind all offices on COA recommendations   and strictly comply with the same.

There are programmed and/or intervening/ urgent activities not programmed in budget which need to be augmented in order to effectively deliver the basic services to the constituents.
observations.reason: Management was unable to review the PPAs before the finalizing the annual budget since there were still augmentations through realignment happened in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the OIC-City Treasurer to set-up realistic income estimates and intensify collection of real property taxes.  Send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their unpaid obligations and conduct a massive tax information campaign and coordinate with the barangay officials in collecting these taxes.
observations.managementAction:
Conduct massive Tax information campaign thru media coverage, personal communication and public auction of delinquent real properties and to update local revenue code.

Tax collection is affected by shutdown companies, VOS, properties affected by calamities resulting to incapacity of taxpayers, properties within boundary disputes. The proposed ordinance updating the local revenue code is at the committee level of the SP.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer’s Office conducted various activities/programs to increase the collection of real property taxes. However, despite of the different activities implemented, still the city collected only a minimal amount of the real property tax receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the City Treasurer to impose the 1% ad valorem tax for idle lands to generate additional revenue for the city. Moreover, management should instruct the City Assessor to expedite the inventory taking of all idle lands located within the territorial jurisdiction of the city to ensure that all taxpayers are taxed fairly and equitably.
observations.managementAction: Imposition of tax collection for idle lands based on final list submitted by the City Assessors Office.
observations.reason: The city imposed the tax for idle lands but there were no actual collections made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management consider the following:

a. Establish M & E tool and devise a reporting system on the progress/status of PPAs using the performance indicators, targets and baselines with an analysis of expected outputs, objectives and goals;
b. Develop standards of preparation and reporting of the accomplishments that would facilitate review vis-à-vis the development plans of the city; and
c. Conduct regular periodic meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned and to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Establish performance monitoring tool and develop templates for reporting on regular basis.
observations.reason: The performance monitoring tool was not institutionalized and templates for reporting were not introduced to the different offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Local School Board to:
a. Stop allowing the utilization of SEF for ineligible expenses; and
b. Prioritize the funding of programs, projects and activities stipulated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the Annual and Supplemental Budget for SEF.
observations.managementAction: Directed the Local School Board, City Accounting to comply with allowable expenditures.
observations.reason: Management already stopped the charging of ineligible expenses to SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management compel the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the procurement monitoring report within the time frame stipulated in Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 so that monitoring and evaluation of the city’s compliance to the provisions of the Government Procurement Act can be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Directed the BAC and GSO to undertake the recommendations in accordance with existing guidelines.
observations.reason: PMRs were submitted to the GPPB and the same were posted in their official website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the department heads to strictly observe proper procurement planning to avoid frequent revisions of the PPMPs and to fast track the implementation of the PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Directed all offices to strictly observe procurement planning and PPMP revision every six (6) months only when necessary for urgent programs.
observations.reason: All the offices of the city were able to prepare and submit the PPMP but they were not religiously following the schedule of procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the GAD Focal Point System to carefully examine the GAD Plan in accordance to the prescribed processes and procedures and to formulate gender-responsive PPAs to address gender issues and concerns based on a gender analysis undertaken. Moreover, management should also allocate in the authorized GAD Plan and Budget PPAs that are mainstreamed in various offices and properly align it with the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction:
Creation of the Focal Point System with Focal person per office.

To assign from the focal persons/ focal point system some full-time personnel to permanently handle the supervision and management of all GAD activities.
observations.reason: The GAD Plan was prepared not fully compliant to the JMC Circular No. 2013-1 1 which resulted to some PPAs which did not address specific gender issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Point to submit to COA the Accomplishment Reports with supporting documents to substantiate the listed accomplishments. Moreover, management should strictly comply the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 dated September 19, 2011 on the required posting of GAD Accomplishment Reports in the LGU website and in the three (3) designated areas in the city.
observations.managementAction: Directed concerned offices to comply with recommendations.
observations.reason: GAD Accomplishment Reports were not supported with documentary evidence but not posted in the LGU website and three (3) conspicuous places.

observations.managementAction: Management directed the concerned offices to comply the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Reports on the Sources and Utilization of Local Risk Disaster Reduction and Management Fund were submitted quarterly instead of monthly. There were no monitoring reports made by the CDRRM Officer that could aid in assessing the implementation of the different PPAs charged to the LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management limit the amount given as medical assistance to its officials so as not to affect the delivery of basic services to its constituents.  Henceforth, local officials should strictly adhere to the principles governing the usage of public funds provided in Section 305 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction:
Direct the concerned offices to adhere to existing guidelines   and no more processing of similar transactions. To establish guidelines on the range/limit of medical assistance to officials and 
To request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to enact and ordinance setting the guidelines/ range/ limits of medical assistance to LFU Bislig officials, on untoward incidents in line with the performance of official functions.
observations.reason: Management did not process similar transaction to avoid depletion of government funds and established guidelines on the maximum amount to be extended to its LGU officials and employees who are in crisis situation.
